data.title: Vanguard to offer its clients access to third-party crypto ETF
data.description:
The global asset manager, Vanguard, is preparing to allow access to crypto ETFs on its brokerage platform. Reportedly, the company has begun laying the groundwork and holding external discussions due to strong client demand for digital assets and a shifting regulatory environment. The mutual fund giant with $10 trillion in assets under management has, until […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/vanguard-to-offer-third-party-crypto-etf/

data.title: U.S. Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs Experience Significant Outflows
data.description: According to TechFlow, as of September 26, monitoring data from Lookonchain reveals that ten U.S. Bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs) have seen a net outflow of 1,790 BTC. Additionally, nine Ethereum ETFs have experienced a net outflow of 50,655 ETH. These figures highlight a notable movement in cryptocurrency assets within the ETF market.

data.title: Bitcoin's Dominance in Crypto Portfolios Highlighted in CoinGecko Report
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, CoinGecko has released a report titled 'Bitcoin's Dominance in Cryptocurrency Portfolios,' revealing that nearly half of retail investors consider Bitcoin (BTC) as the most important asset in their holdings. Notably, 20.4% of participants have portfolios almost entirely centered around Bitcoin. The most popular strategy involves holding Bitcoin along with a small number of altcoins, adopted by 28.0% of market participants, indicating a preference for Bitcoin valuation while waiting to shift towards riskier altcoins.

Conversely, a quarter of market participants view Bitcoin as less important, favoring altcoins instead. Among these, 17.3% allocate funds equally between altcoins and BTC, while 10.2% focus primarily on altcoins, with Bitcoin playing a minor role, typically pursuing higher risk and higher return strategies.

Additionally, 15.9% of market participants deem Bitcoin completely unimportant, opting to invest solely in altcoins. These investors believe that BTC has limited upside potential compared to other cryptocurrencies or avoid BTC entirely due to other factors.

That’s honestly pretty […]
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/the-first-meme-coin-tied-to-both-bitcoin-and-ethereum-pumpd-could-be-2025s-biggest-sleeper-hit/

data.title: SWIFT Explores Blockchain Messaging with Linea
data.description: According to PANews, Raphaël Bloch, co-founder and editor-in-chief of The Big Whale, clarified on the X platform regarding the recent news about SWIFT's development of stablecoins and on-chain messaging based on Linea. Bloch stated that the Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication (SWIFT) has chosen Linea to test its on-chain messaging model. Currently, around a dozen banks are participating in this phase. However, the stablecoin initiative is only in the 'consideration stage,' and the interbank token project is still under review.

data.title: Analysis: Ethereum is extremely oversold, with the RSI at its lowest level since April lows
data.description: Analysts indicate that Ethereum's RSI (Relative Strength Index) is at its lowest level since ETH hit a low of $1,384 in April. While ETH has risen 189% since then, its future trajectory is uncertain. However, such a low RSI suggests ETH is extremely oversold. Furthermore, over $5.2 billion in ETH options expired today, one of the largest end-of-quarter expiration dates ever. ETH's biggest pain point is around $3,800. Following this settlement, ETH is expected to see further gains.

data.title: Market News: SWIFT is developing stablecoins and "on-chain messaging" based on the Linea network
data.description: According to market news, SWIFT (Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication) is developing stablecoins and "on-chain messaging" functions based on the Linea network.

data.description:

source: https://www.bankless.com/read/news/sec-finra-launch-crypto-dat-insider-trading-probe-wsj

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Experiences Significant Liquidations
data.description:
According to TechFlow, data from Coinglass reveals that the cryptocurrency market witnessed liquidations totaling $849 million over the past 24 hours. Of this amount, long positions accounted for $714 million, while short positions made up $135 million.

Bitcoin saw liquidations amounting to $212 million, and Ethereum experienced $278 million in liquidations. During this period, a total of 209,216 traders faced liquidations, with the largest single liquidation occurring in the ETH-USDT trading pair on a centralized exchange, valued at $19.2582 million.

data.title: Camilla McFarland Joins The Ether Machine as Chief Growth Officer
data.description: According to Foresight News, The Ether Machine has officially announced the appointment of Camilla McFarland as its Chief Growth Officer. McFarland previously held roles at ConsenSys, where she was responsible for marketing, strategy, and operations. She also served as the Head of Financial Technology Marketing for ConsenSys Codefi and has experience in content management at the hedge fund Bridgewater Associates.

data.title: Theta Capital plans to raise $200 million for a new blockchain fund of funds
data.description: According to Bloomberg, crypto investment firm Theta Capital Management plans to raise $200 million for a new blockchain fund-of-funds. The new fund, called Theta Blockchain Ventures V, will invest in 10 to 15 venture capital firms focused on digital assets, targeting a net internal rate of return of 25%. Founded in 2001 and headquartered in Amsterdam, Theta shifted its focus to digital assets in 2018. It currently manages approximately $1.2 billion in assets and recently completed a funding round exceeding $170 million. Across the five funds in the Theta Blockchain Ventures series, Theta's portfolio achieved a net internal rate of return (IRR) of 32.7% from January 2018 to December 2024. Theta's portfolio includes well-known cryptocurrency venture capital firms such as Pantera Capital, CoinFund, Polychain Capital, and Dragonfly.

data.title: Odds of Bitcoin dropping below $100K by 2026 spike to 60%
data.description:
The probability that Bitcoin (BTC) will fall below $100,000 before the start of 2026 has surged on crypto prediction platform Polymarket, reflecting growing bearish sentiment after a turbulent week for digital assets. The market, which poses the question“Will Bitcoin dip below $100k before 2026?”, has seen odds spike from 47% on September 25 to 60% at the time of publication. Bitcoin betting odds for end of 2026. Source: Polymarket Earlier this week, when Bitcoin traded above $117,000, the same contract carried just a 44% chance, with confidence eroding just as quickly as price momentum fades
source: https://finbold.com/odds-of-bitcoin-dropping-below-100k-by-2026-spike-to-60/

data.title: Analysis: The whale who transferred 200,000 ETH did not sell it, and the funds flowed to Aave and Plasma
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, the whale who transferred 200,000 ETH did not sell. 100,000 ETH was transferred to the address "0xc6b...9c7" and subsequently deposited into Aave for staking and yield. Another address, "0xc6b...9c7," deposited 100,000 ETH into Aave, borrowed 1 million USDT, and then deposited it into Plasma for yield. Analysis suggests this ETH may belong to Bitfinex, but it's unclear whether it's user assets or held by Bitfinex itself. Bitfinex is also an investor in Plasma.

data.title: A certain smart money has increased its holdings of WBTC by $1.838 million in the past half hour
data.description: According to Ai Yi, an on-chain analyst, the “smart money that made $5.16 million by shorting BTC when LUNA/UST collapsed” added another $1.838 million of WBTC half an hour ago. As of now, he has spent a total of $11.66 million to build a position of 106.09 WBTC, with a cost price of $109,985.

data.title: A whale shorted BTC and XRP, resulting in losses exceeding $1.2 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale named @qwatio created a new wallet (0x9018) and deposited 4.22 million USDC into Hyperliquid. This gambler shorted 1,366.67 BTC (worth $150 million) with 40x leverage and 2.78 million XRP (worth $7.7 million) with 20x leverage, resulting in losses exceeding $1.2 million. The BTC liquidation price was $110,280; the XRP liquidation price was $3.0665.
